2605.23159.title: Generative AI and the Reorganization of Labor Demand
2605.23159.paper_summary: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to transform work, but less is known about how firms reorganize labor demand as the technology diffuses. Existing research has largely focused on which occupations are exposed to AI or whether exposed jobs decline. We extend this debate by examining whether firms adjust by changing where they hire, what jobs contain, or both. Using a nationwide dataset of job postings in the United States, covering all sectors of the economy, we construct a dynamic, posting-level measure of generative AI exposure with a two-stage large language model pipeline. The pipeline identifies the tasks described in each posting and classifies the extent to which generative AI can perform or assist them. We then decompose changes in aggregate exposure into two margins: reallocation of demand across jobs and redesign of tasks within jobs. We document three main findings. First, generative AI exposure is dynamic rather than fixed, changing substantially over time. Second, labor demand adjusts through both margins. Hiring reallocation explains the largest share of the aggregate decline in exposure, accounting for 52% on average, while within-job redesign becomes increasingly important, accounting for 39.5%. A complementary Oaxaca-Blinder decomposition shows that shifts in occupational composition account for about 90% of the exposure change attributable to observable job characteristics. Third, adjustment differs across the job ladder. Senior jobs adjust earlier and mainly through reallocation, whereas junior jobs adjust through a broader mix of reallocation, redesign, and their interaction. These findings suggest that labor-market adjustment to generative AI is a process of organizational reconfiguration, in which firms reshape both hiring demand and the task architecture of work.
2605.23159.paper_summary_zh: 生成式人工智慧（AI）預期將改變工作，但對於企業在技術擴散時如何重組勞動需求的了解較少。現有研究主要集中於哪些職業受到AI影響或受影響的工作是否會減少。我們通過檢視企業是否通過改變招聘地點、工作內容或兩者來進一步擴展這一辯論。利用涵蓋美國所有經濟部門的全國職位發布數據集，我們構建了一個動態的職位層級生成式AI暴露度測量，採用兩階段大型語言模型管道。該管道識別每個職位描述的任務，並分類生成式AI能夠執行或協助這些任務的程度。然後，我們將總體暴露度的變化分解為兩個邊際：跨職位的需求重新配置和職位內任務的重新設計。我們記錄了三個主要發現。首先，生成式AI的暴露度是動態的而非固定的，隨著時間的推移而顯著變化。其次，勞動需求通過兩個邊際進行調整。招聘重新配置解釋了總體暴露度下降的最大份額，平均佔52%，而職位內的重新設計變得越來越重要，佔39.5%。一項補充的Oaxaca-Blinder分解顯示，職業組成的變化約占可觀察工作特徵所致暴露變化的90%。第三，調整在職位階梯上有所不同。高級職位較早進行調整，主要通過重新配置，而初級職位則通過更廣泛的重新配置、重新設計及其互動進行調整。這些發現表明，勞動市場對生成式AI的調整是一個組織重構的過程，企業在此過程中重塑了招聘需求和工作的任務架構。
2605.23159.author: Fangyan Wang et.al.
2605.23159.authors: Fangyan Wang, Zaiyan Wei, Yang Wang
